description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: build-app and build-image. Use the node:8 Docker image for the build-app job, which caches node_modules, stores the build directory as an artifact that expires in one day, and runs the commands: unset CI, yarn install, cp .env.dist .env, yarn lint, yarn build. Then add a build-image job that runs in the build-image stage, depends on build-app, uses the docker:latest image with the docker:dind service, and executes: docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_JOB_TOKEN registry.gitlab.com || true, echo "Image created at:" > build/meta.txt, date -u >> build/meta.txt, docker pull $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG || docker pull $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:master || true, docker build --tag=$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA --cache-from=$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG --cache-from=$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:master --file=docker/Dockerfile ., docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA, docker tag $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG, docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG.
